info.title: Sourced — hosted corroboration API
info.description:
Corroboration verdicts for claims: how many DISTINCT INDEPENDENT origins report each claim's event, since when, with receipts. Honest by design: undercounts rather than overcounts, syndicated copies collapse to one origin, single-origin claims stay unlabeled, and it never says 'true'.

Two tiers:
- **anonymous** — no key, 60 requests/minute, corroboration is computed within each batch (stateless).
- **API key** (`Authorization: Bearer sk_src_…`) — 600 requests/minute, a persistent event store (firstSeenAt is real; corroboration accumulates ACROSS requests), and an own hash-chained transparency log whose head is anchored daily into public git history.

Keys are currently issued manually: hello@tickwire.news.

Spec: https://sourced.ink · Proof: https://sourced.network · The `/api/v1/*` paths are frozen — v1 never breaks. (`/api/assess` and `/api/verify` remain as aliases.)

get.summary: Machine-readable usage summary (includes your tier when a key is sent)

post.summary: Corroboration verdicts for a batch of claims
post.description: Anonymous: corroboration within the batch. With a key: the call also reads/writes your persistent event store, so the same event reported by a new origin an hour later STILL corroborates, and firstSeenAt is the true first sighting.

clusters.description: Optional pre-grouping: claim id -> origins reporting that same event in this batch (max 100 origins per entry). Duplicates collapse.

200.description: One verdict per input claim, in input order. null = unassessable claim (passes through unlabeled — never an error). `memory` is "batch" (anonymous) or "persistent" (keyed).

401.description: Malformed, unknown, or revoked key. Requests WITHOUT a key are never 401 — anonymous is a supported tier.

post.description: Recomputes every record hash, back-link and sequence number. For human verification prefer the in-browser receipts at https://sourced.network — this endpoint is for programmatic use.

jsonl.description: Alternative: raw JSONL, one record per line.

payload.description: With seq: check an inclusion proof for this payload.

200.description: Valid: { ok: true, length, head, payloadIncluded? } · Broken: { ok: false, badIndex, reason: 'hash mismatch' | 'broken link' | 'sequence gap' }.

get.summary: Read a hosted transparency chain (public — that's the point)

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: The chainId (sha256 of the owning key, truncated — public, unguessable, never reversible).

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: The whole history from genesis — what you need to verify.

200.description: Without month/full: { chainId, name, seq, head, ts, months }. With: { records: LogRecord[] }.

post.description: Commits any JSON payload into your hash-chained log: { payload } → the new LogRecord. Reads are public; writes need the key. Heads are anchored daily into public git history (github.com/Ryan-hasi/sourced/tree/main/anchors) — after that, not even we can rewrite your history unnoticed. Limits: 32 kB payload, 30 appends/minute, 5000 records/month.

payload.description: Any JSON — e.g. a digest of the verdict batch you just published.

401.description: Missing/unknown key — chains are writable only by their owner.

get.summary: Public directory of all hosted chains
get.description: [{ chainId, name, seq, head, ts }] — what the daily anchor workflow reads, and how anyone can independently mirror all heads.

bearerKey.description: API key (sk_src_…). Issued manually for now: hello@tickwire.news.

id.description: Stable report id; used to look up clusters entries.

title.description: The claim in words — event identity derives from it.

origin.description: The source; the unit of independence. Normalize consistently (always 'reuters', never sometimes 'Reuters UK').

publishedAt.description: Upstream publish time; drives the 'breaking' window. Unparseable disables 'breaking' for this claim only.

corroboration.description: Distinct independent origins for this claim's event — within the batch (anonymous) or across all your requests (keyed).

corroboratingSources.description: Receipts: the OTHER origins (never the claim's own). Max 6 by default.

firstSeenAt.description: Anonymous: the request time. Keyed: the TRUE first sighting across your requests.

signal.description: Deliberately no 'true' and no veracity score.

Config.description: Numeric threshold overrides, clamped server-side. Defaults are the honesty contract — tighten freely, loosen knowingly.

payloadHash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized payload (keys sorted recursively).

prevHash.description: Previous record's hash; empty string at genesis.

BadRequest.description: Malformed envelope. Malformed INDIVIDUAL claims never fail a request — they return null verdicts.

RateLimited.description: Per-minute budget exhausted (anonymous: 60/min, keyed: 600/min, chain appends: 30/min). Headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After.
